title: 🦞 OpenClaw | The AI That Actually Does Things
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw. From clearing inboxes to managing calendars, all from your favorite chat app. $ `powershell -c "irm https://openclaw.ai/install.ps1 | iex"`. # Install OpenClaw. # Meet your lobster. $ `powershell -c "& ([scriptblock]::Create((irm https://openclaw.ai/install.ps1))) -InstallMethod git"`. # You built it, now meet it. Companion App (Beta)   Menubar access to your lobster. Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows. Extend capabilities with community skills or create your own. See what OpenClaw can do before you install. 6.8 makes the surface richer and more predictable: Telegram renders tables, lists, and expandable blockquotes with intentional line breaks preserved, WhatsApp honors configured ACP bindings, GLM-5.2 and Claude Haiku 4.5 land in the catalog, /usage gets a real footer renderer, and key-free search providers stop being chosen automatically behind your back. ### OpenClaw 6.6: The Boundary Fails Closed, Fable 5 Lands. It runs on your own machine (Mac, Windows, or Linux) and can execute real tasks—managing files, controlling browsers, sending messages, and automating workflows across apps like WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, and iMessage.

title: 🦞 What is OpenClaw? Your Open-Source AI Assistant for 2026
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Your Open-Source AI Assistant for 2026. Yet that’s precisely what OpenClaw (previously Moltbot and Clawdbot) did—despite the fact that it’s not an offering from one of the frontier AI labs. Rather than offering another chatbot, OpenClaw delivers a true personal AI agent that runs locally, remembers context across conversations, and can actually do things on your machine. If you’ve yet to try it for yourself, or you’re still wondering “What is OpenClaw?”—this guide has you covered. * OpenClaw is a viral open-source AI assistant that acts as a proactive personal agent, connecting AI models with your local files and messaging apps like WhatsApp and Discord to automate tasks around the clock. * The project remains model-agnostic and privacy-focused, allowing you to bring your own API keys for cloud models or run local models entirely on your own infrastructure. It brings together the technology of agents with the data and apps you use on your local machine to serve as a high-powered, high-context AI assistant.

title: 🦞 Introducing OpenClaw - OpenClaw Blog
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

The journey from Clawd to Moltbot to OpenClaw—and why this name is here to stay. Today, I’m excited to announce our new name: **OpenClaw**. **Clawd** was born in November 2025—a playful pun on “Claude” with a claw. **OpenClaw** is where we land. The name captures what this project has become:. ## What OpenClaw Is. OpenClaw is an open agent platform that runs on your machine and works from the chat apps you already use. ## What’s New in This Release. I’d like to thank all security folks for their hard work in helping us harden the project. We’ve released machine-checkable security models this week and are continuing to work on additional security improvements. Over the last few days I’ve worked on adding maintainers and we’re slowly setting up processes so we can deal with the insane influx of PRs and Issues. ## Thank You. To the Claw Crew—every clawtributor who’s shipped code, filed issues, joined our Discord, or just tried the project: thank you.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw. Open-source autonomous AI assistant software. **OpenClaw** is a free and open-source autonomous artificial intelligence agent that can execute tasks via large language models (LLMs), using messaging platforms as its main user interface. Within two months it was renamed twice: first to "Moltbot" (keeping with a lobster theme) on January 27, 2026, following trademark complaints by Anthropic, and then three days later to "OpenClaw" because Steinberger found that the name Moltbot "never quite rolled off the tongue.". The viral popularity of Moltbook coincided with an increase in interest in the project, with the open-source project having 247,000 stars and 47,700 forks "Fork (software development)") on GitHub as of March 2, 2026. Some Chinese developers have adapted OpenClaw to work with the DeepSeek "DeepSeek (chatbot)") family of models and domestic messaging super apps such as WeChat, while companies such as Tencent and Z.ai have announced OpenClaw-based services. OpenClaw bots run locally and are designed to integrate with an external large language model such as Claude, DeepSeek, or one of OpenAI's GPT models.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Scheduled tasks
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Cron is the Gateway's built-in scheduler. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output to a chat channel, a webhook, or nowhere. `openclaw cron create "2027-02-01T16:00:00Z" \ --name "Reminder" \ --session main \ --system-event "Reminder: check the cron docs draft" \ --wake now \ --delete-after-run`. `openclaw cron listopenclaw cron get <job-id>openclaw cron show <job-id>`. `openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>`. Cron task reconciliation is runtime-owned first, durable-history-backed second: an active cron task stays live while the cron runtime still tracks that job as running, even if an old child session row still exists. Once the runtime stops owning the job and a 5-minute grace window expires, maintenance checks persisted run logs and job state for the matching `cron:<jobId>:<startedAt>` run. To require both conditions, use croner's `+` day-of-week modifier (`0 9 15 * +1`), or schedule on one field and guard the other in your job's prompt or command. On `cron edit`, removes the per-job model override so the job follows normal cron model precedence (stored cron-session override, else agent/default model).

title: 🦞 Cron Jobs & Sub-Agents - Introduction to OpenClaw | Stanza
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron Jobs & Sub-Agents. These features let you schedule recurring tasks and spawn child sessions that communicate with each other, enabling sophisticated automation workflows. `// Creating a cron job using the cron tool. // The job is persisted with key: cron:dependency-check`. The `cron.create` call registers a job with a unique `id`, a standard cron `schedule` expression, the `command` to execute, and any `context` the job needs. The job is stored under the key `cron:dependency-check` and will persist across sessions, meaning it continues to run even after you close and reopen OpenClaw. // Returns: [{ id: 'dependency-check', schedule: '0 * * * *', ... const job = await cron.get('dependency-check');. Cron jobs can run in two modes: in the **main session** (where the job's output is added to the next heartbeat turn) or in an **isolated session** (where the job gets its own fresh context window). `// A cron job that spawns a sub-agent for nightly analysis.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.

That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.

title: 🦞 Automation - OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

OpenClaw runs work in the background through tasks, scheduled jobs, inferred commitments, event hooks, and standing instructions. | Send daily report at 9 AM sharp | Scheduled Tasks (Cron) | Exact timing, isolated execution |. | Remind me in 20 minutes | Scheduled Tasks (Cron) | One-shot with precise timing (`--at`) |. | Run weekly deep analysis | Scheduled Tasks (Cron) | Standalone task, can use different model |. ### Scheduled Tasks (Cron) vs Heartbeat. | Dimension | Scheduled Tasks (Cron) | Heartbeat |. Use Scheduled Tasks (Cron) when you need precise timing or isolated execution. ### Scheduled tasks (cron). See [Scheduled Tasks](/automation/cron-jobs). ### Tasks. The background task ledger tracks all detached work: ACP runs, subagent spawns, isolated cron executions, and CLI operations. Heartbeat turns do not create task records and do not extend daily/idle session reset freshness. * [Scheduled Tasks](/automation/cron-jobs) — precise scheduling and one-shot reminders. * [Background Tasks](/automation/tasks) — task ledger for all detached work. * [Task Flow](/automation/taskflow) — durable multi-step flow orchestration.
